last_result.effect: Committing this accepted tactic would change the EasyCrypt proof state and return a post-commit workspace view.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

probe_preview.effect: This preview is speculative only; the committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

structural_transition.decision: Commit this exact accepted tactic only if you want to enter the chosen proof-family phase.
structural_transition.after_commit: The next view should expose the authoritative workbench for the selected proof family.
structural_transition.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.tactic:
have -> : Pr[CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(A), RealOrcls(GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO)))).main
   () @ &m : res] = Pr[MainD(G4(A), FinRO).distinguish() @ &m : res] by byequiv => //; proc; inline *; sim.
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
evidence: Local adapter `G4(A)` normalizes wrapper endpoint `CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(A),RealOrcls(GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO))))` to typed endpoint `MainD(G4(A),FinRO)` before Pr rewrite/split handles.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: pivot.RHS vs goal.Pr#1 share the wrapper root; diff at arg_2 → arg_1. `apply (<pivot_name> <args>).` is a route candidate with the arg from your goal's module expression at arg_2 → arg_1. Legacy/KB hint only: inspect it only after the cu...
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.
ask_manager_for.returns: daemon-verified, committable Pr bridge routes with manager-owned intent payloads

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need pRHL/procedure-equivalence bridge lemma names or context after checking pr_bridge_routes.
ask_manager_for.returns: context-only equiv bridge lemma candidates (names to look up/apply); not a verified Pr route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need local lemma hints before choosing a Pr-level proof route.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and proof-route context

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need exact byphoare/phoare-loop tactic syntax before probing the probability route.

lookup_candidates.use_when: Resolve exact signature for `pr_CCP_OCCP` only when this handle route is being tested.
